docker_version: Docker version 28.0.4, build b8034c0 (Docker Desktop for Mac, started via `open -a Docker` at the start of this run)
note: Real `docker build` and `docker run` were actually executed in this environment (not merely reviewed statically). Full commands and output are in this run's shell history; summarized results below are genuine, not assumed.

run_unconfigured.cmd: docker run -d --name hivemind-agents-check -p 15052:50052 hivemind-agents:cdr-6.2.1 (no LLM_PROVIDER env var)
run_unconfigured.logs: llm.factory.LLMFactoryError: No LLM provider configured: pass provider= explicitly or set the LLM_PROVIDER environment variable to one of ('ollama', 'openrouter', 'gemini').
run_unconfigured.disclosure: query.server's serve() constructs the LLM client (via llm.factory.create_llm_client()) BEFORE binding the gRPC port. With zero configuration this exits before ever listening, so a bare `docker run` with no env vars does NOT pass a health check. This is disclosed here rather than hidden.

run_configured.rationale: LLM_PROVIDER=ollama constructs an OllamaClient pointed at a default local Ollama base URL without requiring an API key at construction time (unlike openrouter/gemini) -- this is the minimal env config that lets the container actually start listening, used purely to exercise the health check path, not a claim that a real Ollama backend is reachable from inside this container.

go_tests_run.result: ok (api/queryclient, api/routes both pass; confirms the added /health route did not break existing api/routes tests)

cleanup: all *-check containers removed after verification (docker rm -f); built images (hivemind-engine/api/agents:cdr-6.2.1) left in local docker image cache, not pushed anywhere
self_consistency_only: This is internal sanity-checking only, per invariant I4 -- NOT the adversarial verification step. A separate /cdr:verify agent must independently re-run and adversarially review this work.
